title: Docugen - each document to have a unique name when you download to pdf
excerpt: Hello - we utilize docu gen for certificate generation and automatic distribution via email through monday.com. Occasionally, we find the need to download the generated PDFs and store them in our SharePoint. However, a c…

posts.author: Eloise2023 (Eloise Cowlishaw)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello - we utilize docu gen for certificate generation and automatic distribution via email through monday.com. Occasionally, we find the need to download the generated PDFs and store them in our SharePoint. However, a challenge arises as all downloaded files carry the same name as the template. Is there a solution to incorporate an individual’s name into the title of each document when downloading the PDF?

posts.author: JulesD (Julie Derevycka)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there Eloise, Did you manage to sort this? As we’d love to do that too!

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml: @anon29275264 you’re a life saver

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml:
@Eloise2023 @JulesD

Thank you both for your note and sorry for the delay.
You probably know that you can name your documents dynamically using information from your board:

DocuGen help center

Name your documents

You can have DocuGen give a fixed name to your generated documents, or give them 'dynamic' names using placeholders. Note the special considerations related to document names at the bottom of this...

Perhaps you can add a column to your board that assigns a serial number to each item, and use this column in the document name? (Unfortunately we don’t support Autonumber columns at the moment.)

Another alternative is to use the undocumented placeholder <<job_id>>. Although it’s long and not exactly pretty, it’s nevertheless a totally unique identifier for your documents.

If you want, you can submit this idea to our community website:

DocuGen Community

Ideas & feature requests

Submit your ideas and feature requests here.

Good luck!

posts.author: JulesD (Julie Derevycka)
posts.cookedHtml: Sami, thanks loads this solves the issue for me!
